OTF shell: footer band vs main row columns — handoff for future chats
======================================================================

Purpose
-------
Explain how the “footer” fits into the Endfield Over-The-Frontier (OTF) layout
mentally model, how it lines up with the horizontal columns above it, and what
is actually implemented in this repo vs still documented only in JS comments.


1) Vocabulary (design space = rem on :root, typically 1rem ≈ 16px)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
• Main canvas row height: `--canvas-h` = 90rem → see `LunaSidebar.css` :root and
  `DESIGN_REM_H` / `CANVAS_H` in `canvasScale.js`.

• Intended OTF footer band height (below the main canvas row): 48rem design
  → `OTF_FOOTER_DESIGN_REM` and `getOtfFooterDesignHeightPx()` in
  `canvasScale.js`. The JS comment there says this should stay in sync with a
  CSS custom property `--page-row-otf-footer-h` in `LunaSidebar.css` :root.

• Stage width (center column): `--stage-w` = 120rem — the fixed “main content”
  column in the horizontal flex row.

• Sidebar shell (`.sidebar-shell.UxzaHe`): sits on the right; collapsed vs
  expanded widths are `--sidebar-collapsed` / `--sidebar-expanded` (and
  `getSidebarShellDesignWidthPx()` in `canvasScale.js`).


2) Horizontal layout: the “other columns” (main canvas row)
----------------------------------------------------------
The reference shell (see `Resize-scaling-notes/CSSReferencefile.txt` and
`jD8MDo-flex-layout-note.txt`) uses a single full-width flex ROW `.n1I9fl`
inside page frame `.VYqscx`:

  [ left grow column ]  [ fixed stage ]  [ right grow column ]

Roughly:
• Left (e.g. `.jD8MDo`) and right (e.g. `.TXNnjm`): flexible strips that share
  extra horizontal space (flex-grow), decorative backgrounds, height 100% of
  the row’s content box.

• Center `.mUiHp_`: fixed width `--stage-w` (120rem in this project’s tokens).
  This is the “stage” — the primary content column. It does not participate in
  horizontal flex-grow the same way; it holds the main UI.

• The Luna sidebar (`.sidebar-shell.UxzaHe` / `.UxzaHe` in the reference) is
  anchored to the right of the stage area (absolute positioning in our CSS). In a
  full OTF tree it visually belongs to the right side of the canvas row: drawer
  + rail stack beside/over the stage, not inside the 120rem stage width.

So “other column” relative to the footer means: everything in that main row
(left flex + stage + right flex + right-docked sidebar) sits ABOVE the footer
band. The footer is not one of those three flex children; it is a separate
horizontal band underneath the whole row (when the full shell is assembled).


3) Vertical layout: where the footer sits
------------------------------------------
Conceptual full page (when integrated like the live OTF page):

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  .VYqscx / .n1I9fl — main canvas row (90rem) │
  │  [ flex | stage 120rem | flex | sidebar ]   │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  OTF footer band (48rem design height)       │  ← full width under the row above
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Relationship to columns:
• The footer row spans the same overall width as the viewport (or scaled
  artboard), not “only under the 120rem stage”. It aligns under the entire main
  row, so it sits under the left/right decorative columns and the stage together.

• `getOtfFooterDesignHeightPx()` exists so host code can reserve space, measure
  offsets, or scale a layout that includes BOTH `CANVAS_H` and the footer (e.g.
  total design height 90rem + 48rem = 138rem if stacked with no gap — exact
  stacking/gaps depend on the final JSX/CSS you add).


4) What this repo actually renders today (important)
-----------------------------------------------------
• `App.jsx` mounts only `<LunaSidebar />` inside `.app-shell`. The component
  returns the sidebar shell + drawer + rail (`LunaSidebar.jsx`); there is no
  `.viewport-fit` / `.artboard` wrapper in App and no footer slot in JSX.

• `LunaSidebar.css` still contains rules for `.viewport-fit`, `.page-frame.VYqscx`,
  `.page-row.n1I9fl`, `.mUiHp_`, etc., for parity when you compose the full OTF
  shell elsewhere or restore the scaled artboard wrapper.

• The CSS custom property `--page-row-otf-footer-h` mentioned in `canvasScale.js`
  is NOT currently defined in `LunaSidebar.css` — the footer is anticipated in JS
  exports but not wired in stylesheet yet. If you add a footer row, define that
  variable (48rem) in :root and use it for the footer band height to match the
  helpers.


5) Files to read or update next
--------------------------------
• `src/luna-sidebar/canvasScale.js` — `OTF_FOOTER_DESIGN_REM`, sidebar width helpers.
• `src/luna-sidebar/LunaSidebar.css` — `--canvas-h`, `--stage-w`, shell/sidebar rules;
  add `--page-row-otf-footer-h` when implementing the footer row.
• `Resize-scaling-notes/jD8MDo-flex-layout-note.txt` — flex row + stage vs grow columns.
• `Resize-scaling-notes/CSSReferencefile.txt` — minified reference `.VYqscx` / `.n1I9fl`.


6) One-line summary
-------------------
The footer is a full-width band below the 90rem main canvas row; the row’s
columns (flex sides + fixed 120rem stage + right-docked sidebar) all live above
it. This repo exports the footer’s design height in JS but does not yet render
the footer or define the matching CSS variable.
